questions.question: Ramses II, famously known as Ramses the Great was a ruler of which country?
questions.explanation: Recently, Switzerland returned to Egypt a piece of a 3,400-year-old statue belonging to the ancient Egyptian pharaoh Ramses II. The fragment had been stolen over three decades ago. Ramses II, famously known as Ramses the Great, governed for over six decades in the 13th century BC. Throughout his reign, he achieved significant military triumphs, spearheaded grand construction initiatives, and remarkably fathered a progeny of over 100 offspring.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry launched the ‘Scheme for Expansion and Modernization of Fire Services in the States’?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Family and Health Affairs

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Home Affairs has initiated the “Scheme for Expansion and Modernization of Fire Services in the States” with a total budget of Rs. 5,000 Crore. A sum of Rs. 500 crore from the overall budget has been allocated to incentivize States based on their implementation of legal and infrastructure-related reforms.

questions.question: Karuvattu Mana Vasudevan Namboothiri, who was seen in the news, is associated with which profession?
questions.explanation: Renowned artist Karuvattu Mana Vasudevan Namboothiri popularly known as artist Namboothiri passed away. Namboothiri illustrated characters of top Malayalam writers such as Thakazhi Sivasankara Pillai, Kesavadev, M T Vasudevan Nair, Uroob, S K Pottekkatt and VKN. He was also a painter, sculptor and art director.

questions.question: Which Department has planned to introduce compulsory quality control and safety standards for various household electrical appliances?

options.label: C
options.text: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

options.label: D
options.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Grievance

answer.label: C
answer.text: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

questions.explanation: The Government of India’s Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has planned to introduce compulsory quality control and safety standards for various household electrical appliances. The targeted appliances are classified under 6 categories. This initiative is aimed at reduction in import of sub-standard products from countries such as China and promote domestic manufacturing.

questions.question: Which institution released the report titled ‘Twenty Years of Child Trafficking Data’?
questions.explanation: The International Organization for Migration (IOM) and the François-Xavier Bagnoud Center for Health and Human Rights at Harvard University collaborated on a report titled ‘From Evidence to Action: Twenty Years of IOM Child Trafficking Data to Inform Policy and Programming.’ According to this report, more than 50% of child trafficking victims are subjected to trafficking within their own countries.

questions.question: Joro spiders, originally from East Asia, are spreading as invasive species in which country?
questions.explanation: Trichonephila clavata, also known as the Joro spiders, originally from East Asia, have started spreading throughout the United States, establishing Georgia as their new habitat. These striking spiders are known for their colorful appearance and the impressive size of their webs. Female Joro spiders can grow up to 3 inches, while males are smaller. Joro spiders are orb weavers, spinning large circular webs.

questions.question: Which company announced a breakthrough in creating Majorana zero modes?
questions.explanation: Majorana zero modes are a theoretical concept that mathematically represents electrons as composed of two distinct parts. Microsoft researchers have announced a breakthrough in creating Majorana zero modes, which will help in advancing quantum computing by enhancing stability and resilience in quantum systems.

questions.question: How many people were lifted out of poverty in India from 2005-06 to 2019-21, as per the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
questions.explanation: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and OPHI released the latest update of the Multidimensional Poverty Index. It revealed that India has witnessed a significant reduction in poverty, with 415 million people lifted out of poverty within a span of 15 years from 2005-2006 to 2019-2021.

questions.question: Pholcodine, is a drug present in which type of medicine?
questions.explanation: India’s drug regulatory authority has planned to issue a warning about the potential risks associated with Pholcodine-containing cough and cold remedies due to concerns of life-threatening allergic reactions. Pholcodine, an opioid cough suppressant found in certain cough medications, has sedative effects and is used to alleviate dry coughs.

questions.question: Which institution released the ‘2023 Employment Outlook’ report?
questions.explanation: According to the OECD’s 2023 Employment Outlook, there is currently limited evidence of notable adverse impacts on employment resulting from AI. The report highlighted that despite the current low adoption rate of AI, the combination of swift advancements, decreasing costs, and the growing pool of skilled AI professionals suggests that OECD economies could be on the verge of an imminent AI revolution.
